UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile on the portfolio website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

bio.description: A brief biography or description of the user.

title.description: The job title of the user.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project showcased in the portfolio.

techStack.description: List of technologies used in the project.

githubUrl.description: Link to the GitHub repository of the project.

liveUrl.description: Link to the live demo of the project.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a skill of the user.

name.description: Name of the skill (e.g., JavaScript, React, Node.js).

category.description: Category of the skill (e.g., Languages, Frameworks, Tools).

projectIds.description: References to Projects. (Relationship: Skill N:N Project)

Experience.title: Experience
Experience.description: Represents a past work experience or internship.

role.description: Job title or role during the experience.

company.description: Name of the company or organization.

description.description: Description of the role and responsibilities.

toolsUsed.description: List of tools and technologies used during the experience.

ContactFormSubmission.title: ContactFormSubmission
ContactFormSubmission.description: Represents a submission from the contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the contact form submission.

name.description: Name of the person submitting the form.

email.description: Email address of the person submitting the form.

message.description: Message content from the contact form.

submissionDate.description: Date and time of the submission.

/projects/{projectId}.description: Stores all projects for the portfolio.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Accessible only to the user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores projects associated with a specific user. Accessible only to the user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the project.

definition.description: Stores skills associated with a specific user. Accessible only to the user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for the skill.

definition.description: Stores experience entries associated with a specific user. Accessible only to the user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: experienceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the experience.

definition.description: Stores achievements associated with a specific user. Accessible only to the user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: achievementId
params.description: The unique identifier for the achievement.

definition.description: Stores contact form submissions. Accessible only to administrators.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contact form submission.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to manage user profiles, projects, skills, experiences, achievements, and contact form submissions for a personal portfolio website. It prioritizes authorization independence by leveraging path-based ownership for user-specific data and segregates data with different access requirements. The structure will look like `/users/{userId}/profile`, `/users/{userId}/projects/{projectId}` and so on. This structure provides clear ownership. Contact form submissions are stored in a separate collection (`/contact_form_submissions`) to allow for different security rules, in this case only accessible to administrators. All dates are stored as strings to maintain compatibility with Javascript and to enforce a predictable schema. The `Skill` entity's `projectIds` property establishes a `N:N` relationship between skills and projects. This design supports QAPs by enabling secure list operations based on ownership and role-based access control, implemented via dedicated collections.
